name: SSIoBC-did Project
observations: PhD research on fully on-chain DID document management system
First complete on-chain DID implementation maintaining W3C compliance
Uses Foundry framework for smart contract development
Solidity 0.8.24 with Shanghai EVM version
Gas-optimized architecture using hash-based lists and EnumerableSet
4-year DID expiration with reuse capability
Multi-method support: did:method0:method1:method2:id structure
Maintains >90% test coverage requirement

name: DidManager Contract
observations: Core contract inheriting from VMStorage and ServiceStorage
Manages DID lifecycle: creation, expiration, controller updates
Uses mapping(bytes32 => uint) for expiration dates
Fixed controller array of length 5 (CONTROLLERS_MAX_LENGTH)
Self-sovereign by default (empty controllers = owner controls)
Generates DIDs using keccak256(methods, random, tx.origin, block.prevrandao)
Calculates ID hash as keccak256(methods, id) for storage indexing

name: VMStorage Contract
observations: Abstract contract for verification method storage
Uses EnumerableSet for O(1) add/remove/contains operations on VM IDs
Supports multiple VM relationships: authentication, assertion, key agreement, etc.
Position-hash mapping for VM validation system
Stores VMs with expiration timestamps and ethereum address validation
Supports publicKeyMultibase, blockchainAccountId, or ethereumAddress
Relationship flags stored as bytes1 with bitwise operations

name: ServiceStorage Contract
observations: Abstract contract for service endpoint storage
Uses EnumerableSet for efficient service ID management
Fixed arrays for service types and endpoints (SERVICE_MAX_LENGTH)
Supports creation, update, and deletion of services
Uses service namespace for hash-based storage separation
Position tracking for efficient enumeration and events

name: W3CResolver Contract
observations: Optional contract for on-chain W3C DID document resolution
Translates internal storage format to W3C-compliant JSON structure
Handles verification method relationships mapping
Supports expired/unexpired filtering in resolution
Formats DID strings according to W3C specification
Provides resolveVm and resolveService for individual component resolution

name: Foundry Development Environment
observations: Uses forge build, forge test, forge coverage commands
Configured with 20,000 optimizer runs for gas efficiency
Shanghai EVM version target
Custom remappings for @src/, @interfaces/, @test/, @script/
OpenZeppelin contracts integration
File system permissions for read-write access
Storage layout output enabled for analysis

name: Test Architecture
observations: SharedTest.sol base class with common utilities and constants
Event testing using vm.recordLogs() and log analysis
Helper functions: _createDid(), _createVm(), _deployNewDidManager()
Comprehensive test constants for DID methods, VM types, relationships
Test files follow ContractName.t.sol naming convention
>90% coverage requirement with tracking across versions
Gas reporting integration for optimization validation

name: TempFileManagement
observations: Always generate non-code related files (analysis outputs, size comparisons, reports, logs) in .temp/ folder
Examples of temp files: sizes_before.txt, sizes_after.txt, gas reports, coverage outputs, deployment logs
.temp/ folder is excluded from version control but preserved locally for development
Follows consolidation over proliferation principle for file organization
Part of SSIoBC-did project's organized file management system

name: GitignoreOrganization
observations: Organized with emoji sections for better readability and maintenance
Categories: Build&Compilation, Development&Broadcasting, Documentation, Environment, Dependencies, AI&Tooling
Each section uses descriptive emojis and clear grouping
Follows existing AI section pattern with 🤖🧠 emojis
.temp/ folder excluded under AI&Tooling section
